rationale: v2 redo. Captain correctly called the v1 list (22 specialties) rubbish — it folded too aggressively. Medicine has ~50 boarded specialties per ABMS, the GMC, and the EU UEMS list. v2 follows real-world clinician identity: every primary residency / EU-recognised specialty gets its own card. Surgery is split into the 9 surgical specialties that are themselves boarded (general, vascular, cardiothoracic, neurosurgery, orthopedic, plastic, transplant, pediatric, trauma). Ophthalmology, ENT, urology — long-time mistakes in v1 — each get their own card. Subspecialties (e.g. interventional cardiology) stay at L3.

subspecialties.name: Cardiology
subspecialties.tagline: Heart, circulation, electrophysiology, prevention.
covers: coronary disease, heart failure, arrhythmia, valve disease, preventive cardiology
JACC, Circulation, EHJ

subspecialties.name: Medical Oncology
subspecialties.tagline: Cancer systemic therapy, immuno-oncology, trials.
covers: medical oncology, hematologic and solid tumors, immunotherapy, targeted therapy, clinical trials
JCO, Lancet Oncol, Nat Cancer

subspecialties.name: Radiation Oncology
subspecialties.tagline: Radiotherapy planning, SBRT, brachytherapy, proton therapy.
covers: radiation oncology, SBRT, proton therapy, brachytherapy, FLASH RT

subspecialties.name: Neurology
subspecialties.tagline: Stroke, epilepsy, MS, neurodegenerative, neuromuscular.
covers: stroke, epilepsy, MS, Parkinson's, Alzheimer's, ALS, neuromuscular disease, headache, sleep medicine

subspecialties.name: Psychiatry
subspecialties.tagline: Mood, psychosis, addiction, psychopharmacology, child psych.
covers: mood disorders, schizophrenia, anxiety, addiction medicine, child & adolescent psychiatry, geriatric psychiatry, psychopharmacology
Am J Psychiatry, Lancet Psychiatry, JAMA Psychiatry
explicitly_excludes: cognitive psychology basic → Brain & Cognitive Sciences

uncovered_concerns: Still no separate card for Forensic Medicine, Aerospace Medicine, Diving Medicine — too small to occupy a card; fold under Occupational & Environmental Medicine or Emergency Medicine.

Pediatric subspecialties (pediatric cardiology, pediatric nephrology, etc.) live as L3 children of Pediatrics rather than separate L2 cards — they're populated by the same practitioner pool with a pediatric lens.
Hospice Medicine kept under Palliative Care rather than a separate card.
Public Health Medicine (a UK/EU specialty) is folded under Public Health at L1.
